mechanics.raw_text:
PARANOIA 
The Troubleshooters are an elite class of citizens of proven merit, (That is to say, they consist largely of former Infrareds who proved their loyalty by turning comrades in to The Computer as traitors) Troubleshooters are formally assigned to service groups for trai ing, but in fact they are under the direct con- tual of The Computer. Most mission groups in- clude Troubleshooters from several service jroups, providing each team with an effective mixture of various backgrounds, skills, and, abilities, Troubleshooters are assigned the challeng- ing missions for which none of the other sere ice group staffs are particularly well-suited (or eager). Betause oftheir preven loyalty, they are foften assigned to missions where sensitive issues of security and public policy are concerned 
Individual Troubleshooters are organized in- to mission groups. Sometimes a successful mmission group remains together for a series of missions, Sometimes Troubleshooters are reas- signed to new groups for no apparent reason. Sometimes individual Troubleshooters are assigned to solo missions, Usually The Computer selects a group's mis- sion, though successful groups are sometimes offered a choice of missions. Mission leaders are usually designated at the mission briefing, ‘along with minor staff roles like loyalty officer, 
TH WILL BE VE SAFE AND LOTS OF FUN FOR ALL OF yOu, 
‘weapons offices vehicle officer, robotics officer, ‘medical officer, hygiene officer, and so on. Mis- sion briefings are usually conducted by an i ‘mediate superior, though they are sometimes ‘conducted by The Computer through one of its many terminals or by courierdelivered writ- ten orders. In emergencies ahi ‘Troubleshooter or other powerful citizen may ‘order Troubleshooter teams on his own authority when communications with The ‘Computer have been compromised. ‘The missions assigned to Troubleshooters are imariably difficult and dangerous. This means that player characters have many opportunities to distinguish themselves in the service of The ‘Computer. If they survive, they may expect to ‘advance in status more rapidly than the laborers, technicians, bureaucrats, and ‘managers of the regular service groups.
